title: A Delsarte-Style Proof of the Bukh-Cox Bound
author_string: Mark Magsino, Dustin G. Mixon, Hans Parshall
abstract: The line packing problem is concerned with the optimal packing of points in real or complex projective space so that the minimum distance between points is maximized. Until recently, all bounds on optimal line packings were known to be derivable from Delsarte's linear program. Last year, Bukh and Cox introduced a new bound for the line packing problem using completely different techniques. In this paper, we use ideas from the Bukh--Cox proof to find a new proof of the Welch bound, and then we use ideas from Delsarte's linear program to find a new proof of the Bukh--Cox bound. Hopefully, these unifying principles will lead to further refinements.
